title: list
text: list A string comprising one or more items, in which items are separated by a subfield separator character. A list can contain the possible values for a field (known as a ValRep list). How a list is presented to users depends on the type of widget, and the properties for the field in which the list is displayed. Each list contains zero or more "items" and can be treated either as an "indexed list" or as an "associative list". An associative list can be said to contain a ValRep list. Each ValRep [item] consists of a value and a representation. A list is often used to set the list of properties or values for a field and its widget. Any item in a list (or the whole list) can be "empty"; the value of an item can be "NULL" (not "empty"). Related Links associative list A list in which each list item consists of a pair of associated values representing a key and value, or a value and its representation. In Uniface, list items are separated by a Gold separator, such as Gold-semicolon (;). For example, "CA=Canada;DE=Germany;US=United States". indexed list A list in which each item is identified by an integer representing the absolute position of the item in the list. ValRep list A Uniface list for a field, representing the possible values and their representations. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
